title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy

intro.heading: Understanding ACT and Its Effectiveness
intro.body: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) is a form of psychotherapy that uses acceptance and mindfulness strategies mixed in different ways with commitment and behavior-change strategies, to increase psychological flexibility. Developed within a context of Relational Frame Theory, ACT is unique in its approach to psychological issues, offering a path for individuals to embrace their thoughts and feelings rather than fighting or feeling guilty for them.

features.title: Cognitive Diffusion
features.desc: Techniques to alter undesirable functions of thoughts and other private events, rather than trying to eliminate or alter their form or frequency.

features.title: Acceptance
features.desc: Allowing thoughts to occur without the struggle to change them. It’s learning to make room for painful feelings, sensations, and urges, instead of resisting them.

features.title: Mindfulness
features.desc: Interacting with others in the group setting helps improve communication skills, empathy, and relationship-building.

features.title: Self as Context
features.desc: Being present in the current moment in a non-judgmental way, allowing a deeper connection with oneself.

features.title: Values Clarification
features.desc: Helping individuals identify and connect with the things that are truly important and meaningful to them in their lives.

features.title: Skill Development
features.desc: Setting goals according to one’s values and carrying them out responsibly, leading to a more fulfilled life.

features.title: Flexibility
features.desc: ACT improves the ability to contact the present moment and change or persist in behavior that serves one’s personally chosen values.

features.title: Reduces Distress
features.desc: It is effective in reducing the impact of unpleasant thoughts and feelings, helping with various aspects of mental health, including anxiety, depression, and stress.

features.title: Adaptable
features.desc: ACT can be applied to a wide range of psychological issues and is suitable for diverse age groups and cultural backgrounds.

cta.heading: ACT is particularly valuable for those who have struggled with traditional change-based treatments. It encourages an accepting relationship with oneself and promotes a commitment to positive change, leading to a richer, more meaningful existence, even in the face of difficulty.
cta.body: Your therapist will carefully explain and tailor treatment methods in your plan, ensuring they align with your specific needs and therapeutic goals.

excerpt: A mindfulness-based approach that helps you make room for difficult thoughts and feelings while committing to the life you value.
seoDescription: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) in Philadelphia — a mindfulness-based approach to build psychological flexibility, reduce distress, and create lasting change.
